Let's look at the actions available.
1. `rotate`: Rotates the heading of a node `?n` by `?r`. Sets `rotating` flag and starts the first pass cursor at `?n`.
2. `rotate-first-pass`: Updates the next node in the string (`?n2`) based on the rotation. It clears old position/heading, sets new heading, moves cursor to `?n2`.
3. `rotate-first-pass-end`: Handles the end of the first pass (when `?n2` is END-NODE). Sets cursor for second pass.
4. `rotate-second-pass`: Updates the position of the next node (`?n2`) based on the previous node's (`?n1`) position and heading. Sets cursor to `?n2`.
5. `rotate-second-pass-end`: Ends the second pass, clears `rotating`.

...
`h_k` = `h_{k-1}` + Rot(n_k).
Where `+` is composition of rotations.

So `h_k` = Up + Rot(n1) + Rot(n2) + ... + Rot(n_k).
